name: Sarasota
tagline: Gulf Coast Beaches, Zero State Tax, and Cultural Richness
description: Sarasota pairs some of the most beautiful beaches in America with zero state income tax, making it a top-tier domestic FIRE destination. The city punches well above its weight culturally, with world-class performing arts, the Ringling Museum, and a thriving food scene. While Florida housing costs have climbed, Sarasota still offers better value than Miami or Naples with arguably better quality of life.

quickFacts.visa: No visa required for US citizens
quickFacts.safetyRating: High – consistently ranked among Florida's safest metro areas
quickFacts.climate: Tropical with warm, humid summers and mild, dry winters

housing.description: 1BR apartment in Gulf Gate, Bee Ridge, or North Sarasota

dining.description: Casual waterfront spots and happy-hour specials 2x/week; mostly cooking at home

healthcare.description: ACA marketplace Silver plan; Florida premiums can be high without subsidies

transportation.description: Paid-off car with basic insurance; car is essential in Sarasota

entertainment.description: Free beach days at Siesta Key, sunset walks, public parks; occasional museum visit

1M.lifestyle: A one-bedroom apartment in Gulf Gate or North Sarasota -- away from the expensive waterfront -- cooking most meals at home, and a paid-off car. Healthcare on an ACA Silver plan is a significant expense in Florida, and this budget does not leave much room for surprises. The trade-off is access to Siesta Key beach and public parks at no cost, and zero state income tax on every withdrawal. This is a lean budget for Sarasota, but workable if you are disciplined.

housing.description: 2BR condo in downtown Sarasota or a townhome in Palmer Ranch with pool access

dining.description: Regular dining at St. Armands Circle and downtown restaurants 3-4x/week

healthcare.description: ACA Gold plan with dental and vision; manageable out-of-pocket costs

transportation.description: Reliable car, full coverage insurance including comprehensive for hurricane season

entertainment.description: Sarasota Opera and ballet tickets, kayaking, Ringling Museum membership, gym

2M.lifestyle: A two-bedroom condo downtown or a townhome in Palmer Ranch with pool access, dining out several times a week at St. Armands Circle and downtown restaurants, and a reliable car with full coverage including hurricane-season comprehensive. You can afford Sarasota Opera tickets, a Ringling Museum membership, and a kayak outing now and then. Good health coverage through an ACA Gold plan rounds things out. Zero state income tax helps this budget go further than the same amount in most coastal metros.

housing.description: 3BR waterfront condo or a home in Bird Key or Harbor Acres

dining.description: Fine dining at Indigenous, Bijou Cafe, and waterfront spots; regular wine dinners

healthcare.description: Premium private health insurance with concierge-lite features

transportation.description: Late-model car, premium insurance, occasional boat rental for island hopping

entertainment.description: Season tickets to performing arts, boating, golf club access, spa treatments

3M.lifestyle: A three-bedroom waterfront condo or a home in Bird Key, regular dining at restaurants like Indigenous and Bijou Cafe, and season tickets to the performing arts. You can afford premium health insurance, a golf club membership, occasional boat rentals for island hopping, and a late-model car. The combination of zero state income tax and Sarasota's strong cultural scene makes this a comfortable, well-rounded coastal life with room for regular travel.

housing.description: Luxury waterfront home on Bird Key, Lido Key, or a Gulf-front condo on Longboat Key

dining.description: Private chef for dinner parties, fine dining multiple nights per week, yacht club dining

healthcare.description: Concierge medicine, executive health screenings, premium private insurance

transportation.description: Luxury vehicle, boat slip rental, occasional private car service

entertainment.description: Yacht club membership, private golf club, patron-level arts sponsorships, deep-sea fishing

domesticHelp.description: Weekly house cleaning, pool service, landscaping, hurricane prep service

luxuryExtras.description: Quarterly travel, boat ownership costs, art collecting, spa memberships

5M.lifestyle: A waterfront home on Bird Key or Lido Key, a housekeeper who comes weekly, concierge medical coverage, and a nice car. You dine at the city's best restaurants regularly, rent a boat slip for weekend Gulf outings, and fly business class for longer trips. A private golf club membership and patron-level arts involvement are within budget. Florida's zero state income tax means none of your withdrawal goes to state taxes, which adds meaningful value at this spending level.

housing.description: Beachfront estate on Siesta Key or Casey Key, or a luxury compound on Longboat Key

dining.description: Full-time personal chef, catered events, exclusive wine dinners, yacht catering

healthcare.description: Top concierge medical practice, executive wellness, specialist access in Tampa

transportation.description: Multiple luxury vehicles, yacht captain, private car service, chartered flights from SRQ

entertainment.description: Major arts philanthropy, international travel, private island excursions, exclusive events

domesticHelp.description: Full-time estate manager, housekeeper, personal assistant, boat captain, pool/grounds crew

luxuryExtras.description: Yacht ownership, first-class international travel, real estate investments, philanthropy

10M.lifestyle: A beachfront home on Siesta Key or Casey Key, a cook who comes in several times a week, full-time domestic help, and top-tier concierge healthcare with specialist access in Tampa. You travel first class, keep multiple cars, and own or lease a boat for Gulf outings. Dining out at the best restaurants is routine, and there is substantial margin for charitable giving, arts patronage, and a second property if desired. Zero state income tax keeps every dollar of your withdrawals intact.
